ECC | | Fan Temp Perf Pwr:Usage/Cap| Memory-Usage | GPU-Util Compute M. | | | | MIG M. | |===============================+======================+======================| | 0 GeForce RTX 207... Off | 00000000:01:00.0 On | N/A | | 0% 45C P8 21W / 215W | 271MiB / 7973MiB | 14% Default | | | | N/A | +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+ +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | Processes: | | GPU GI CI PID Type Process name GPU Memory | | ID ID Usage | |=============================================================================| | 0 N/A N/A 1209 G /usr/lib/xorg/Xorg 35MiB | | 0 N/A N/A 1932 G /usr/lib/xorg/Xorg 106MiB | | 0 N/A N/A 2052 G /usr/bin/gnome-shell 82MiB | | 0 N/A N/A 3151 G /usr/lib/firefox/firefox 2MiB | | 0 N/A N/A 3217 G /usr/lib/firefox/firefox 2MiB | | 0 N/A N/A 3376 G ...AAAAAAAAA= --shared-files 28MiB | +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+ Conflicting installation sudo /usr/local/cuda-X.Y/bin/uninstall_cuda_X.Y.pl Runfile installation download CUDA Version: 11.2 wget https://developer.download.nvidia.com/compute/cuda/11.2.0/local_installers/cuda_11.2.0_460.27.04_linux.run install sudo sh cuda_11.2.0_460.27.04_linux.run follow CUDA11.2.0 DOCUMENT and zhihu test installation nvcc nvcc -V nvcc: NVIDIA (R) Cuda compiler driver Copyright (c) 2005-2020 NVIDIA Corporation Built on Mon_Nov_30_19:08:53_PST_2020 Cuda compilation tools, release 11.2, V11.2.67 Build cuda_11.2.r11.2/compiler.29373293_0 sample cd ~/NVIDIA_CUDA-11.2_Samples/; make; cd 1_Utilities/deviceQuery/; ./deviceQuery ./deviceQuery Starting... install conda install cudatoolkit conda install numba test installation (run code test.py ) from numba import cuda def cpu_print(): print("print by cpu.") @cuda.jit def gpu_print(): # GPU核函数 print("print by gpu.") def main(): gpu_print[1, 2]() cuda.synchronize() cpu_print() if __name__ == "__main__": main() Installation Tensorflow-gpu install cudnn conda install cudnn install tensorflow-gpu

docs.text: install conda install cudatoolkit conda install numba test installation (run code test.py ) from numba import cuda def cpu_print(): print("print by cpu.") @cuda.jit def gpu_print(): # GPU核函数 print("print by gpu.") def main(): gpu_print[1, 2]() cuda.synchronize() cpu_print() if __name__ == "__main__": main()

docs.title: Install Tensorflow-gpu

docs.text: terminology (May 27, 2021) D: the lower mantle starting at 1000-km depth D': mesosphere (between 1000-km and D") D": the lowermost mantle MORB: midocean-ridge basalts Ma: million years PKJKP: seismic wave that traverses the IC as a shear wave P'P': wave that goes through the core and reflects off of the opposite side of the earth Preface and Philosophy (May 27, 2021) object: a better understanding of origin, structure, composition and evolution of our planet multidisciplinarity is more essential than ever seismologist: apt to interpret velocity anomalies in terms of temperature variations in a homogeneous, isotropic model petrologists: variations in mineralogy, crystal orientation or partial melt content counts more than temperature in the seismic velocity isotope geochemists: 650-km discontinuity, tend to interpret the chemical data in terms of ancient isolated reservoirs; exotic basalts are assumed to be from the CMB; MORB are viewed as a unique component of the mantle, rather than as an average composition of a heterogeneous population. convection theoretician: treats the mantle as a homogeneous fluid or as a 2-layered system, driven by temperature-induced buoyancy. Bias: rocks and magmas only represent shallow mantle and crust; seismic data is controlled by many factors, not only temperature-density current Earth paradigms from different disciplines: seismologists prefer a whole-mantle convection, while geochemists prefer layered convection deep-mantle features may be convectively isolated from upper-mantle processes. part 2: a dynamic planet (May 27, 2021) Plates are separated by boundaries and meet at triple junctions. In view of thermodynamics, the plates are driven by the thermal and density variations in the mantle . Cooling plates and sinking slabs can also be regarded as driving themselves, and driving convection in the underlying mantle. The cold stiff outer shell of earth is the active element and the template; the underlying convective mantle is passive. Ridge-trench-slab system is the main plate tectonic cycle. chap 4: the outer shell of the earth (June 1, 2021) Definition of plates is partly differnent from that of the lithosphere. It breaks at suture zones, fracture zones, subplate boundaries.
docs.title: terminology (May 27, 2021)

docs.text: D: the lower mantle starting at 1000-km depth D': mesosphere (between 1000-km and D") D": the lowermost mantle MORB: midocean-ridge basalts Ma: million years PKJKP: seismic wave that traverses the IC as a shear wave P'P': wave that goes through the core and reflects off of the opposite side of the earth
docs.title: terminology (May 27, 2021)

docs.text: object: a better understanding of origin, structure, composition and evolution of our planet multidisciplinarity is more essential than ever seismologist: apt to interpret velocity anomalies in terms of temperature variations in a homogeneous, isotropic model petrologists: variations in mineralogy, crystal orientation or partial melt content counts more than temperature in the seismic velocity isotope geochemists: 650-km discontinuity, tend to interpret the chemical data in terms of ancient isolated reservoirs; exotic basalts are assumed to be from the CMB; MORB are viewed as a unique component of the mantle, rather than as an average composition of a heterogeneous population. convection theoretician: treats the mantle as a homogeneous fluid or as a 2-layered system, driven by temperature-induced buoyancy. Bias: rocks and magmas only represent shallow mantle and crust; seismic data is controlled by many factors, not only temperature-density current Earth paradigms from different disciplines: seismologists prefer a whole-mantle convection, while geochemists prefer layered convection deep-mantle features may be convectively isolated from upper-mantle processes.
docs.title: Preface and Philosophy (May 27, 2021)

docs.text: Plates are separated by boundaries and meet at triple junctions. In view of thermodynamics, the plates are driven by the thermal and density variations in the mantle . Cooling plates and sinking slabs can also be regarded as driving themselves, and driving convection in the underlying mantle. The cold stiff outer shell of earth is the active element and the template; the underlying convective mantle is passive. Ridge-trench-slab system is the main plate tectonic cycle.
docs.title: part 2: a dynamic planet (May 27, 2021)

docs.text: Definition of plates is partly differnent from that of the lithosphere. It breaks at suture zones, fracture zones, subplate boundaries.
docs.title: chap 4: the outer shell of the earth (June 1, 2021)

docs.text: Geodynamics in 2021 spring, lectured by Prof. Leng definition Geodynamics is a subfield of geophysics dealing with dynamics of the Earth. It applies physics, chemistry and mathematics to the understanding of how mantle convection leads to plate tectonics and geologic phenomena such as seafloor spreading, mountain building, volcanoes, earthquakes, faulting and so on. overview Geodynamics is generally concerned with processes that move materials throughout the Earth. In the Earth's interior, movement happens when rocks melt or deform and flow in response to a stress field. deformation of rocks elastic: < yield strength & << relaxation time The magnitude of the stress cannot exceed the yield strength of a material, and the time scale of the stress cannot approach the relaxation time of the material . If stress exceeds the yield strength of a material, bonds begin to break, which can lead to ductile or brittle deformation. ductile: > yield strength & = relaxation time Ductile deformation happens when the temperature of a system is high enough so that a significant fraction of the material microstates are unbound. Examples include bending of the lithosphere under volcanic islands or sedimentary basins, and bending at oceanic trenches. brittle: > yield strength & < relaxation time When strain localizes faster than these relaxation processes can redistribute it , brittle deformation occurs. thermodynamics The most important thermodynamic variables are temperature and pressure. Within the upper lithosphere, brittle deformation is common. After the brittle-ductile transition zone, ductile deformation becomes dominant. Forces The main motive force behind stress in the Earth is provided by the energy from radioisotope decay, friction, and residual heat . Negative thermal buoyancy of the oceanic plates is the primary cause of subduction and plate tectonics, while positive thermal buoyancy may lead to mantle plumes, which could explain intraplate volcanism . Methods Data includes field observations, geodesy, radiometric dating, petrology, mineralogy, drilling boreholes and remote sensing techniques . Numerical modeling Forward modeling to: reproduce a specific observation produce basic fluid dynamics Basic fluid dynamics modelling can further be subdivided into instantaneous studies , which aim to reproduce the instantaneous flow in a system due to a given buoyancy distribution, and time-dependent studies , which either aim to reproduce a possible evolution of a given initial condition over time or a statistical (quasi) steady-state of a given system. Basic SI unit & constant density rayleigh number A picture of Geodynamics (Layer cake or plum pudding ?)

docs.text: Subduction zone 650 km & 2800 km (WE Pacific plate) driving force: temperature difference & density difference subduction factory: 1. feed igneous oceanic crust, ocean bottom sediment, lithosphere mantle into the factory 2. output gas, fluid, magma, associated continental crust material and ore belt 3. process by dehydration melting and metaporphism Earth is the only known planet existing subduction zone.
docs.title: Subduction zone

docs.text: Plate Tectonics The World Atlas names seven major plates: African Antarctic Eurasian Indo-Australian North American Pacific South American. How to define a plate vertically & horizontally? Tectonic plates are gigantic pieces of the Earth's crust and uppermost mantle, i.e. pieces of the lithosphere . The lithosphere, which is the rigid outermost shell of a planet (the crust and upper mantle), is broken into tectonic plates. The types of plate boundaries convergent divergent transform what is Wilson Cycle ? The Wilson Cycle is a model that describes the opening and closing of ocean basins and the subduction and divergence of tectonic plates during the assembly and disassembly of supercontinents. Phases of Wilson cycle: From ten o'clock position clockwise: (10) initial pre-drift extension, (12) rift-to-drift phase, initial opening of an oceanic basin, (2 and 4) seafloor spreading, widening of the basin, (6) subduction of oceanic lithosphere, closure of the basin, (8) continent-continent collision
docs.title: Plate Tectonics

docs.text: African Antarctic Eurasian Indo-Australian North American Pacific South American.

docs.text: Geodynamics in 2021 spring, lectured by Prof. Leng definition Geodynamics is a subfield of geophysics dealing with dynamics of the Earth. It applies physics, chemistry and mathematics to the understanding of how mantle convection leads to plate tectonics and geologic phenomena such as seafloor spreading, mountain building, volcanoes, earthquakes, faulting and so on. overview Geodynamics is generally concerned with processes that move materials throughout the Earth. In the Earth's interior, movement happens when rocks melt or deform and flow in response to a stress field. deformation of rocks elastic: < yield strength & << relaxation time The magnitude of the stress cannot exceed the yield strength of a material, and the time scale of the stress cannot approach the relaxation time of the material . If stress exceeds the yield strength of a material, bonds begin to break, which can lead to ductile or brittle deformation. ductile: > yield strength & = relaxation time Ductile deformation happens when the temperature of a system is high enough so that a significant fraction of the material microstates are unbound. Examples include bending of the lithosphere under volcanic islands or sedimentary basins, and bending at oceanic trenches. brittle: > yield strength & < relaxation time When strain localizes faster than these relaxation processes can redistribute it , brittle deformation occurs. thermodynamics The most important thermodynamic variables are temperature and pressure. Within the upper lithosphere, brittle deformation is common. After the brittle-ductile transition zone, ductile deformation becomes dominant. Forces The main motive force behind stress in the Earth is provided by the energy from radioisotope decay, friction, and residual heat . Negative thermal buoyancy of the oceanic plates is the primary cause of subduction and plate tectonics, while positive thermal buoyancy may lead to mantle plumes, which could explain intraplate volcanism . Methods Data includes field observations, geodesy, radiometric dating, petrology, mineralogy, drilling boreholes and remote sensing techniques . Numerical modeling Forward modeling to: reproduce a specific observation produce basic fluid dynamics Basic fluid dynamics modelling can further be subdivided into instantaneous studies , which aim to reproduce the instantaneous flow in a system due to a given buoyancy distribution, and time-dependent studies , which either aim to reproduce a possible evolution of a given initial condition over time or a statistical (quasi) steady-state of a given system. Basic SI unit & typical constants density: 3000 SI tectonic stress: 10 Mpa (1 pa = 1 N/m2; 1 Mpa = 1e6 pa) Young's Modulus for typical rocks: 70 Gpa magnitude for strain in the lithosphere: 1e-4 Possion's ratio: 0.25 heat flux: 65 +-1.6 mWm-2 for continent; 101 +- 2.2 mWm-1 for ocean thermal conductivity k: Wm-1K-1 A picture of Geodynamics (Layer cake or plum pudding ?)
